knowledge_evidence.title: Conv1d
knowledge_evidence.signature: class torch.nn.Conv1d(in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=1, padding=0, dilation=1, groups=1, bias=True, padding_mode='zeros', device=None, dtype=None)
knowledge_evidence.description: Applies a 1D convolution over an input signal composed of several input planes.

parameters.in_channels: Number of channels in the input image
parameters.out_channels: Number of channels produced by the convolution
parameters.padding: Padding added to both sides of the input. Default: 0
parameters.groups: Number of blocked connections from input channels to output channels. Default: 1
parameters.bias: If True , adds a learnable bias to the output. Default: True

knowledge_evidence.title: BatchNorm1d
knowledge_evidence.signature: class torch.nn.BatchNorm1d(num_features, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True, device=None, dtype=None, *, bias=True)
knowledge_evidence.description: Applies Batch Normalization over a 2D or 3D input.

parameters.num_features: number of features or channels C C C of the input
parameters.eps: a value added to the denominator for numerical stability. Default: 1e-5
parameters.momentum: the value used for the running_mean and running_var computation. Can be set to None for cumulative moving average (i.e. simple average). Default: 0.1
parameters.affine: a boolean value that when set to True , this module has learnable affine parameters. Default: True
parameters.track_running_stats: a boolean value that when set to True , this module tracks the running mean and variance, and when set to False , this module does not track such statistics, and initializes statistics buffers running_mean and running_var as None . When these buffers are None , this module always uses batch statistics. in both training and eval modes. Default: True
parameters.bias: If set to False , the layer will not learn an additive bias (only relevant if affine is True ). Default: True

knowledge_evidence.title: Linear
knowledge_evidence.signature: class torch.nn.Linear(in_features, out_features, bias=True, device=None, dtype=None)
knowledge_evidence.description: Applies an affine linear transformation to the incoming data: y = x A T + b y = xA^T + b y = x A T + b .

parameters.bias: If set to False , the layer will not learn an additive bias. Default: True

paper.title: Solar Power Forecasting Using CNN-LSTM Hybrid Model

research_evidence.title: Solar Power Forecasting Using CNN-LSTM Hybrid Model

plan.summary: Implement a 1D convolutional neural network classifier with the specified architecture, configuration, and a forward-pass shape test.

files.purpose: Define hyperparameters and layer specifications for the model
responsibilities: Store constants such as in_channels, out_channels, kernel_sizes, num_classes
Provide a clear configuration structure for easy modification

files.purpose: Implement the 1D CNN classifier using the configuration
responsibilities: Define a nn.Module subclass that builds the layers according to config
Implement the forward pass with Conv1d -> BatchNorm1d -> ReLU -> MaxPool1d -> Conv1d -> BatchNorm1d -> ReLU -> AdaptiveAvgPool1d -> Linear
Import and use values from config.py

files.purpose: Verify that the model produces the expected output shape
responsibilities: Create a dummy input tensor with shape (batch, 2, 1024)
Run a forward pass and assert that output shape is (batch, 5)

assumptions: Default PyTorch initialization is used for all layers
No training loop or dataset code is required
unresolved_questions: Determine whether bias should be disabled for Conv1d layers (default True)
Define exact testing framework (e.g., unittest, pytest) for shape test

artifacts.content:
# Configuration constants for the 1D CNN classifier

# Input dimensions
IN_CHANNELS = 2
INPUT_LENGTH = 1024

# Number of output classes
NUM_CLASSES = 5

# Convolutional layer 1
CONV1_IN_CHANNELS = IN_CHANNELS
CONV1_OUT_CHANNELS = 32
CONV1_KERNEL_SIZE = 7
CONV1_BATCHNORM_FEATURES = 32

# Convolutional layer 2
CONV2_IN_CHANNELS = CONV1_OUT_CHANNELS
CONV2_OUT_CHANNELS = 64
CONV2_KERNEL_SIZE = 5
CONV2_BATCHNORM_FEATURES = 64
assumptions: Default PyTorch initialization is used for all layers
No training loop or dataset code is required

artifacts.content:
# Import necessary modules
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

import config

class CNN1DClassifier(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super(CNN1DClassifier, self).__init__()
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv1d(config.CONV1_IN_CHANNELS, config.CONV1_OUT_CHANNELS, kernel_size=config.CONV1_KERNEL_SIZE)
        self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm1d(config.CONV1_BATCHNORM_FEATURES)
        self.relu1 = nn.ReLU()
        self.pool = nn.MaxPool1d(kernel_size=2)
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv1d(config.CONV2_IN_CHANNELS, config.CONV2_OUT_CHANNELS, kernel_size=config.CONV2_KERNEL_SIZE)
        self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm1d(config.CONV2_BATCHNORM_FEATURES)
        self.relu2 = nn.ReLU()
        self.adaptive_pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool1d(1)
        self.fc = nn.Linear(config.CONV2_BATCHNORM_FEATURES, config.NUM_CLASSES)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.relu1(self.bn1(self.conv1(x)))
        x = self.pool(x)
        x = self.relu2(self.bn2(self.conv2(x)))
        x = self.adaptive_pool(x)
        x = x.view(x.size(0), -1)  # flatten
        x = self.fc(x)
        return x
assumptions: Default stride=1 and padding=0 for Conv1d layers
Default bias=True for Conv1d and Linear layers
Input tensor shape (batch, 2, 1024) is compatible with the defined architecture

artifacts.content:
import torch
from model import CNN1DClassifier

def test_forward_shape():
    # Create a dummy input tensor with shape (batch, 2, 1024)
    batch_size = 4
    x = torch.randn(batch_size, 2, 1024)
    # Instantiate the model
    model = CNN1DClassifier()
    # Run forward pass
    output = model(x)
    # Assert that the output shape is (batch, 5)
    assert output.shape == (batch_size, 5), f"Expected shape ({batch_size}, 5), got {output.shape}"
    print("Forward pass test passed.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    test_forward_shape()
assumptions: batch size of 4 for dummy input
model.py is importable and defines CNN1DClassifier
the model's forward pass returns a tensor of shape (batch, 5) when given input (batch, 2, 1024)
